title: pistolochia bulbosa subsp. marschalliana
intro: Pistolochia bulbosa subsp. marschalliana, also known as the Marschall's pistilroot, is a plant species in the family Staphylocarpaceae. This plant is used in traditional medicine, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The medicinal properties of Pistolochia bulbosa subsp. marschalliana have been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, providing relief from pain and inflammation, as well as supporting the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal benefits.
